_description: Sanitized structural example of the error handler workflow. Node parameters, registry references, model identifiers, and fix catalog entries are redacted. Node sequence and role annotations reflect actual architectural structure.
name: Error Handler — Redacted Example

nodes.role: Entry point. Receives structured failure context from the orchestrator's error-router node. Context includes error type, error message, failed node type, agent domain, input payload schema, and execution ID. Runs asynchronously — does not block the originating request path.

inputs.agent_domain: string — domain of the failing agent

nodes.role: Looks up the received error_type against the error type registry. Returns the error class (transient | permanent | recoverable), retry eligibility, max_retries, and escalation policy. If the error_type is not found in the registry, marks as 'unclassified' and routes to Path B.

Routes to Path A if a known_fix_pattern exists for the classified error type. Routes to Path B if known_fix_pattern is null or error class is unclassified. Permanent errors with retry_eligible=false skip both paths and route directly to escalation.
Retrieves the full fix pattern definition from the fix catalog by pattern ID. Fix patterns are deterministic — no LLM call is made on Path A.

fix_pattern_types.retry_with_backoff: Wait configured delay, re-attempt failed operation unchanged
fix_pattern_types.registry_key_repair: Look up undefined key in fallback registry partition; substitute and re-execute
fix_pattern_types.credential_refresh: Trigger credential refresh via engine credential manager, then re-execute
fix_pattern_types.cache_bypass: Invalidate specific cache key, force fresh execution, repopulate
fix_pattern_types.payload_sanitize: Apply declared sanitization transform to input payload before re-execution

nodes.role: Applies the retrieved fix pattern to the failure context. Produces a corrected execution payload or modified execution parameters. Does not re-execute yet — fix is applied to a staging context first.

Validates the corrected payload against the failing agent's declared input contract before re-execution. This prevents a malformed fix from producing a second failure. If validation fails, routes to escalation — not to retry.

Re-runs the original failed workflow with the validated corrected payload. Tracks retry count against the contract's max_retries limit. On success, routes to result-logger. On failure, decrements retry count and returns to catalog-lookup if retries remain, or escalates if exhausted.

Assembles the structured repair context to submit to the repair model. Values are excluded — only structure and schema are included. This scopes the model's context to what is strictly necessary and reduces hallucination surface.

context_fields: contract_ref (agent contract for the failing component)

nodes.role: Submits the structured failure context to the repair model. Model returns a proposed fix — either a corrected payload transform, a parameter substitution, or a node configuration change. Model and prompt template are redacted.

Validates the AI-proposed fix against the agent's declared contract before application. Same validation gate as Path A — AI-generated fixes are not exempt from contract enforcement. If validation fails, decrements repair retry count. If max repair retries exhausted, routes to escalation.

When AI-assisted repair produces a successful re-execution, this node adds the fix to the catalog with a human_review_pending flag. After a configurable number of successful applications without regression, the fix graduates to a confirmed catalog entry (Path A) for future occurrences.

Reached when all recovery options are exhausted, when a permanent error is classified, or when a proposed fix fails contract validation. Produces a structured escalation record and routes to the notification channel based on escalation_policy. immediate_escalation alerts synchronously; log_and_notify batches to a review queue.

escalation_record_schema.recommended_action: string — from repair model or catalog

nodes.role: Logs the final outcome of the recovery attempt to the error log data store. Emits structured observability events at each decision point — error captured, path selected, fix applied, validation result, re-execution result, retry count consumed, escalation triggered. These events feed the Error Recovery dashboard.

execution_paths.path_a: failure-capture-trigger → error-classifier → routing-branch → catalog-lookup → fix-applicator → contract-validator → re-executor → [success: result-logger] | [failed/exhausted: escalation-handler]
execution_paths.path_b: failure-capture-trigger → error-classifier → routing-branch → context-builder → ai-repair-model → proposed-fix-validator → re-executor → [success: catalog-promotion-handler → result-logger] | [failed/exhausted: escalation-handler]

design_notes: The error handler runs asynchronously. The user receives a failure acknowledgment from the orchestrator immediately; recovery does not block the response path.
Contract validation is applied to every proposed fix — both catalog-sourced (Path A) and AI-generated (Path B). A fix that produces an invalid payload is escalated, not applied.
Retry counts are consumed across both Path A and Path B attempts within a single failure event. The counter does not reset between paths.
AI-generated fixes that succeed are promoted to the fix catalog with a human review flag. This makes the catalog self-expanding over time.
Permanent errors (retry_eligible=false) bypass both recovery paths entirely and route directly to escalation.
